revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23987730427|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fr:Adachi Myokengu]][[ja:御祖神社]][[simple:Adachiyama Myoken Shrine]]




{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|name=Adachi Myokengu|native_name=足立山妙見宮| religious_affiliation = Shinto
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|image                 = File:Adachi Myokengu.jpg
| deity                 = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}
|consecration_year=770<ref name="https" />|prefecture= {{ill|Kokurakita-ku|qid=Q1042291|1=Kokurakita-ku, Kitakyūshū}}|coordinates= {{Coord|33.862528|N|130.903028|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
{{nihongo|{{ill|Adachi Myokengu|en:draft|Mioya Shrine|en|draft:Adachi Myokengu|ja|御祖神社|1=Adachi Myokengu|2=ja|3=御祖神社|4=fr|5=Adachi Myokengu|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Adachiyama Myokengu|1=Adachi Myokengu|4=fr|5=Adachi Myokengu|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Adachiyama Myokengu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Myokengu|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Myokengu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Myokengu|13=User:Immanuelle/Adachi Myokengu|qq=}}|足立山妙見宮|4=lit: Mount Adachi Myoken Palace|qq=}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kitakyushu|en|Kitakyushu|qid=Q188806|lt=Kitakyushu|1=Kitakyushu}}.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Ltd.|first=TheGridNet|title=About Kitakyushu|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:WoLOoc0OwRYJ:https://kitakyushu.only.site/en/info&cd=14&hl=en&ct=clnk&gl=ca|access-date=2023-10-01|website=The Kitakyushu Grid|language=en|qq=}}</ref> It is also called {{Nihongo|Mioya Shrine|御祖神社|1=Adachi Myokengu|2=ja|3=御祖神社|4=fr|5=Adachi Myokengu|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Adachiyama Myokengu|1=Adachi Myokengu|4=fr|5=Adachi Myokengu|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Adachiyama Myokengu|Mioya-jinja|lit: Divine{{efn|御 is the character in [[Amaterasu]]|qq=}} Ancestor Shrine|qq=}}  
It is dedicated to [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]], who is sometimes known by the Buddhist name {{ill|Myōken|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|qq=}}<ref name="Amenominakanushi">{{cite web|last1=Mori |first1=Mizue |title=Amenominakanushi |url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=38 |access-date=2020-11-07 |website=Encyclopedia of Shinto |publisher=Kokugakuin University|qq=}}</ref>. The two different names are from the two different names. [[fr:Adachi Myokengu]][[simple:Adachiyama Myoken Shrine]]
It was built by {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Kiyomaro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wake no Kiyomaro|qq=}}, a Japanese noble man after he stopped {{ill|Dōkyō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō|qq=}} from becoming {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. He was exiled to {{ill|Mount Adachi|ja|足立山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Adachi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Adachi|1=Adachiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11636998}} and buiilt the shrine on it after wild boars saved him<ref name="https" />. He founded the shrine in 770<ref name="https">https://qrutto.jp/wp-content/themes/qrutto/files/10_toyonokuni_guidebook_english.pdf</ref>
{{ill|Shimogamo Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogamo Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogamo Shrine|qq=}} has a similar formal name {{Nihongo|"Kamo-mioya Shrine'"|賀茂御祖神社|''Kamo-mioya jinja''|qq=}}. While some words only sound similar in Japanese but have different characters, this one is common in characters too.<ref>Richard, Ponsonby-Fane. (1964) '' Visiting Famous Shrines in Japan,'' pp. 1-118.</ref>
People Hike up Mount Adachi on a route that goes by it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mount Adachiyama - Myoken Shrine Loop|url=https://www.alltrails.com/trail/japan/fukuoka/mount-adachiyama-myoken-shrine|access-date=2024-03-29|website=AllTrails.com|language=en|qq=}}</ref> 
{{Commons Category|Mioya Shrine
}}
